warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Charges obtained via finite-difference Mulliken scheme (PySCF): neutral, cation (charge +1, doublet), and anion (charge −1, doublet) single-point calculations on the user-supplied geometry (no re-optimization). All three SCF calculations converged; f+ and f− each sum to 1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied). Integrity check: trustworthy = true. Most electrophilic site: C4 (β-carbon), f+ = 0.64232102. Most nucleophilic site: O1, f− = 0.39388616. The dominant f+ at C4 and large positive dual descriptor (+0.55495) are consistent with acrolein's well-known Michael-acceptor reactivity. No anomalous warnings from the integrity checks.
